root_text.an5.152:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato suṇantopi saddhammaṁ abhabbo niyāmaṁ okkamituṁ kusalesu dhammesu sammattaṁ.
root_text.an5.152:1.3: Kathaṁ paribhoti, kathikaṁ paribhoti, attānaṁ paribhoti, duppañño hoti jaḷo eḷamūgo, anaññāte aññātamānī hoti.
root_text.an5.152:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato suṇantopi saddhammaṁ abhabbo niyāmaṁ okkamituṁ kusalesu dhammesu sammattaṁ.
root_text.an5.152:2.1: Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato suṇanto saddhammaṁ bhabbo niyāmaṁ okkamituṁ kusalesu dhammesu sammattaṁ.
root_text.an5.152:2.3: Na kathaṁ paribhoti, na kathikaṁ paribhoti, na attānaṁ paribhoti, paññavā hoti ajaḷo aneḷamūgo, na anaññāte aññātamānī hoti.
root_text.an5.152:2.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato suṇanto saddhammaṁ bhabbo niyāmaṁ okkamituṁ kusalesu dhammesu sammattan”ti.

translation_text.an5.152:1.1: “Mendicants, someone with five qualities is unable to step into the sure path with regards to skillful qualities even when listening to the true teaching.
translation_text.an5.152:1.3: They disparage the talk, the speaker, or themselves. They’re witless, dull, and idiotic. They think they know what they don’t know.
translation_text.an5.152:1.4: Someone with these five qualities is unable to step into the sure path with regards to skillful qualities, even when listening to the true teaching.
translation_text.an5.152:2.1: Someone with five qualities is able to step into the sure path with regards to skillful qualities when listening to the true teaching.
translation_text.an5.152:2.3: They don’t disparage the talk, the speaker, or themselves. They’re wise, bright, and clever. They don’t think they know what they don’t know.
translation_text.an5.152:2.4: Someone with these five qualities is able to step into the sure path with regards to skillful qualities when listening to the true teaching.”
